series_description: Detailed reports of comprehensive payments to school districts based on school finance calculations.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years ; Electronic: Retain for 5 years

series_description: Projection of financial condition (stability) of Ohio School districts for current and next fiscal year.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years in office

series_description: Forms use to record special education data for submission to EMIS (Education Management Information System). Federal EDGAR rule 100b.734 specifies 5 year
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years ; Paper: Retain 5 years

series_description: School district equipment for inventory purposes, pursuant to
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years ; Electronic: Retain five years

series_description: State plans for the administration of vocational education for the State of Ohio.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years and

series_description: Evaluations of Disadvantaged Pupil Program Funds (DPPF) ) to assist with Ohio’s plan by ensuring schools have resources to create and implement programs.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years ; Paper: Retain 5 years

series_description: Evaluations submitted by selected school districts on approved Urban/Rural Demonstration Projects.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain five years ; Paper: Retain five years

series_description: Periodic reports of Emergency School Advancement Loans (ESAL); Annual Reports of ESAL (Summary), and Year-End Borrowing (Spending Reserve); Historical Perspective Borrowing by Ohio school districts.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain ten years ; Paper: Retain ten years

series_description: Periodic reports of Emergency School Advancement Loans (ESAL); Annual reports of ESAL (Summary); ESAL applications, support documents, loan approval and receipt, repayment schedules, monitoring reports, and necessary notes and communications.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until paper is converted to machine readable format, check for accuracy and completeness prior to discarding the paper. ; Electronic: Retain 5 years

series_description: Field reports by personnel of school visitation and other activities.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in operating areas 5 years

series_description: Essex School Applications - Applications submitted by 60 finalists. Institutes for Gifted and Talented - Proposals submitted by universities, correspondence regarding institutes, records of meetings with university coordinators, and evaluations of institutes. Midwest Talent Honors Program - Computer printout of names of finalists and correspondence pertaining to program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office three years

series_title: Financial Report of the Board of Educati
series_description: Data collection document that contains the revenue and expeditures of each public school district in Ohio. Data is used for statistical purposes as well as being basis for report to the federal governement.
retention_statement: Paper: One year after collection

series_description: State department annually generated a five-year report of resources, expenditures, liquidity and borrowing. Consists of a manual, table and graphs of the above topics of all Ohio school districts.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years in the office

series_title: Single Audit Reports for School Districts
series_description: This records series includes all records related to single audit review process and the final reports for school districts.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years after all issues in question are resolved. ; Electronic: Retain five years after all issues in question are resolved.

series_description: Data collection document that contains the name, address & phone numbers of each school building and district office in Ohio. This data is the basis for the Ohio Educational Directory and for creation for distribution.
retention_statement: Electronic: 10 years

series_title: Schools Receiving Funds for Building Ass
series_description: The Legislature periodically allocates specific funding for schools to receive money for building assistance. Files include: Application, Department Survey, Architectural drawings, schematic drawings, contracts, bank account information, change, deduct,
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until project is closed out

series_description: Reports generated using data collection from school districts that are not published. These forms are used to answer questions relative to the data. Most reports are at the school district level. Records may be produced on paper or microfilm.
retention_statement: Microform: Permanen ; Paper: Permanen

series_description: Records submitted by Ohio school districts investing interim funds pursuant to Section 135.142 of the Ohio Revised Code.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain five years ; Paper: Retain five years

series_description: Roster, records, receipts formerly known as Distributive Education Clubs of America.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years ; Paper: Retain 5 years

series_description: Reports of monitors of school districts falling under Sections 3313.4810 and 3313.488 of the Ohio Revised Code.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain ten years, contact State Archives for review and possible transfer or disposal.. ; Paper: When paper is converted to machine readable format, check for accuracy and completeness prior to discarding the paper

series_title: Financial Analytics, Five-Year Forecast Notifications, District Written Plans
series_description: Financial analysis of district financial reporting including the five-year forecast review, deficit and written plan requirement notifications, correspondence, financial analysis of five-year forecasts and staffing analysis of school districts
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years ; Electronic: Retain 5 years

series_description: Reporting Forms on Local Programs
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in operating areas 5 years

series_description: Includes payment requests, vouchers, invoices, and other data regarding federal grant programs.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain five years after grant closure ; Paper: Retain five years after grant closure

series_title: School District Appropriations and Amended Certificates
series_description: Collection of school district appropriations and official and amended certificates of anticipated revenue.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years ; Electronic: Retain five years

series_title: Disadvantaged, Hanicapped, Work-study, and Community Based Organization Proposals, Reports, and Affi
series_description: Disadvantaged, handicapped, work-study, and community based organization proposals, reports, and affidavits
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in operating areas 5 years

series_description: Includes project activity narrative, budget data, assurances, and student participation data.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years after grant closes, and after state audits have been conducted, audit reports released, and audit resolutions issued or resolved ; Electronic: Retain five years after grant closes, and after state audits have been conducted, audit reports released, and audit resolutions issued or resolved

series_description: Data collection document that contains the school year calendar for the last year just completed and for the upcoming year by school building. Data is used for statistical purposes.
retention_statement: Paper: One year after collection

series_title: Quarterly Reports to the Controlling Board
series_description: Involves records of Emergency School Advancement Loan (ESAL) approval, repayment, and interest on a quarterly basis.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain ten years. When paper is converted to machine readable format, check for accuracy and completeness prior to discarding the paper. ; Electronic: Retain ten years

series_description: Contains Professional Development COS Development and Approval records. Copies of required curriculums filed by the boards of educations of school districts in the State of Ohio.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years ; Electronic: Retain five years

series_description: Maintenance of Effort Records Allocation Data for School Districts
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years ; Paper: Retain five years

series_description: Monies that are distributed to school districts under various state and federal programs. These files include spreadsheets and reports of amounts of monies allocated to school districts.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years ; Electronic: Retain 5 years

series_description: Schedule of assigned hours and shifts to be filled and who will fill those hours.
retention_statement: Paper: Fiscal Year of Record Plus 2 years

series_description: Applications offered by students seeking work on campus.
retention_statement: Paper: 2 years

series_description: Records required to distribute and keep a supply of controlled prescription medications.
retention_statement: Paper: 2 years after use

series_description: Records and books used to compile student grades.
retention_statement: Paper: 3 years ; Electronic: 3 years

series_description: Records to include rental agreements , rental rates for using OSD property
retention_statement: Paper: 4 years

series_description: Inventory of all food supplies including kind , amount and dates purchased and dates to be used by.
retention_statement: Electronic: Until superseded ; Paper: Until superseded

series_description: Food Service Records: Menus, Food Production, Milk Sold, Students Served.
retention_statement: Paper: 4 years provided audited

series_description: Textbook/Workbook Inventory
retention_statement: Paper: Until superseded provided audited

series_description: Form used to certify the destruction or transfer of records according to a state approved records retention schedule.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in operating area until paper is converted to machine readable format, check for accuracy and completeness prior to discarding the paper. ; Electronic: Retain permanently or until of no legal value.

series_description: Copies of audit reports issued by the State Inspector General, Auditor of State or Federal government.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 7 years ; Electronic: Retain 7 years

series_description: Original office files containing case information on schools awarded a charter, changes to a charter school and school's administrative appeal concerning their charter revocation. These may contain include, but not limited to; application briefs, memos, compliance documents, correspondence and hearing officer decisions.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years ; Paper: When paper is converted to machine readable format, check for accuracy and completeness prior to discarding the paper

series_description: Correspondence from the Superintendent of Public Instruction dealing with significant aspects of the Board and Department of Education. Correspondence includes information concerning agency policies, procedures, program, financial and personnel matters.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 4 years, allow State review for possible transfer, and

series_description: Accounting Records to include: Telephone and Fax bills, Per diem forms, Payroll reports, In-State and Out-of-State travel expense reports, Expenditure reports, phone logs and attendence reports.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until audited by AOS, audit report is released, and all discrepancies are resolved

series_description: Budget and related material associated with the State Board of Education and Department of Education.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 1 year after the end of biennium, the destroy.

series_description: Pertinent documents related to the appointment of State Board of Education members.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain original until a copy is to DAS

series_description: File contains contact information, employment eligibility verification, civil service application and other employment documents.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until the end of Board Member's term, retain 5 years thereafter, the review by State .

series_description: Audiotape recordings of monthly State Board of Education Meetings.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain if office until minutes are transcribed for accuracy and completeness, retain for 5 years thereafter, and

series_description: Approved Minutes of official meetings of the State Board of Education of Ohio.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain Permanently.

series_description: These records document a school's participation in the following EdChoice Scholarship, Autism Scholarship, Cleveland Scholarship and/or Jon Peterson Scholarships. These records indicate how many years they have participated, how many students they serve, tuition schedules, school handbooks, attendance and admission requirements and payments.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain five years, provided the records have been audited by the Auditor of State and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_description: All USDA Child Nutrition Program sponsors must complete a sponsor application with supporting documents. Supporting documents include but are not limited to: sponsor access form, demonstrations of financial viability, budgets, vending agreements, menus and menu certification, inspections and operating licenses, enrollment data, training documentation, and federal taxation documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain sponsor applications for three years plus the current year. If an agency is terminated for cause, retain until removed from the National Disqualified List or State Disqualified List (a minimum of seven years) and/or until any outstanding debt is paid to USDA; if an appeal is filed ; Electronic: Retain sponsor applications for three years plus the current year. If an agency is terminated for cause, retain until removed from the National Disqualified List or State Disqualified List (a minimum of seven years) and/or until any outstanding debt is paid to USDA; if an appeal is filed

series_title: Correspondence from the Executive Director and the State Superintendent
series_description: Correspondence from the Executive Director or State Superintendent of Public Instruction dealing with significant aspects of the Straight A Fund Governing Board. Correspondence includes information concerning the Straight A Fund policies, procedures, or program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years, allow State Archives review for possible transfer, and

series_title: High School Equivalence Transcript Requests or Duplicate Diploma Requests
series_description: These include requests for a duplicate copy of a diploma or for a High School Equivalence (GED) transcript. These requests may come from third parties.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for six months ; Electronic: Retain for six months

series_title: Accounting Records, Budgets, and Financial Materials
series_description: Any materials relating to the Straight A Fund finances or disbursement of grant funds.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years and until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved. Review by State before disposal. ; Electronic: Retain 5 years and until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved. Review by State Archives before disposal.

series_title: Appointment and Application Materials for Advisory Council, Grant Advisors, and Scorers
series_description: Documents submitted by the members of the advisory council, grant advisors, and scorers: including but not limited to applications, resumes, and conflict of interest forms. Training materials distributed to advisory council, grant advisors, and scorers.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years. ; Electronic: Retain 5 years.

series_title: Straight A Fund Grant Applications and Scoring Documents
series_description: Grant application materials and scoring documents for the Straight A Fund.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years

series_description: The adopted minutes of Straight A Fund Governing Board meetings.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years;

series_title: Meeting Materials from Straight A Fund Governing Board Meetings
series_description: This file contains all materials distributed at the Straight A Fund Governing Board meetings.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years;

series_description: Score challenge records include Form L-55, the notification that a student wants to challenge their High School Equivalence (GED ) results, as well as the results of the challenge.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain two years ; Paper: Retain two years

series_description: Litigation Reports created by the Attorney General''s office and Chief Legal Counsel provide updates for pending litigation against ODE, the State Board of Education, etc.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for five years

series_description: Internal legal research memoranda regarding various aspects of education law.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain ten years ; Electronic: Retain ten years

series_description: Files relating to referee referrals in teacher/contract termination matters.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain one year. ; Paper: Retain one year. Retain paper in operating area until imaged and indexed. Once confirmed the paper copy has been checked for accuracy, quality and completeness

series_description: Permanent Agreements are contracts between agencies and the Ohio Department of Education outlining specific rights and regulations relative to USDA Child Nutrition Programs.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain permanent agreement until it is superseded. If an agency is terminated for cause, retain until removed from National Disqualified List or State Disqualified List (a minimum of seven years) and/or until any outstanding debt is paid to USDA; if an appeal is filed ; Electronic: Retain permanent agreement until it is superseded. If an agency is terminated for cause, retain until removed from National Disqualified List or State Disqualified List (a minimum of seven years) and/or until any outstanding debt is paid to USDA; if an appeal is filed

series_description: Review of the general and critical areas of a sponsoring agency''s Child Nutrition Program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain review files for three years plus the current year or until next review cycle. If an agency is terminated for cause, retain until removed from National Disqualified List or State Disqualified List (a minimum of seven years) and/or until any outstanding debt is paid to USDA; if an appeal is filed ; Electronic: Retain review files for three years plus the current year or until next review cycle. If an agency is terminated for cause, retain until removed from National Disqualified List or State Disqualified List (a minimum of seven years) and/or until any outstanding debt is paid to USDA; if an appeal is filed

series_description: Agreements between the community school sponsors and the governing authority of the community school.
retention_statement: Paper: Three years past the date on which the contract expires; is superseded by a new contract; or is made void by operation of law. ; Electronic: Three years past the date on which the contract expires; is superseded by a new contract; or is made void by operation of law.

series_description: Agreements between community school sponsors and the Ohio Department of Education, pursuant to 3314.015 of the O.R.C.
retention_statement: Electronic: Three years past the date of the expiration of the contract. ; Paper: Three years past the date of the expiration of the contract.

series_description: Various Federal grants are awarded to eligible Child Nutrition Program sponsors. The Ohio Department of Education, Office for Child Nutrition provides oversight to the operation of the grant on a state level. Records include but are not limited to accepted and denied applications, surveys, scoring matrices, supporting documentation, and all official correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three years plus the current year after the conclusion of the grant. ; Electronic: Retain for three years plus the current year after the conclusion of the grant.

series_title: Formal Written Complaints, Due Process Hearings and Appeals Case Files
series_description: The case files of due process hearing requests and state level appeals. 34 C.F.R. 300.507 (Filing a due process complaint) and 34 C.F.R. 300.514 (Finality of decision; appeal; impartial review). The case files for formal written complaints (state complaints) filed under 34 C.F.R. 300.152 (Minimum state complaint procedures).
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain five years ; Paper: Retain five years

series_title: District Net Indebtedness Consents, Notifications and Approvals of ORC 133.06
series_description: Requests from school districts for consent to issue net indebtedness in accordance with RC 133.06 including: ballot consent to exceed the 4% limitation of its tax valuation, but not more than 12% (special needs); 133.06(H) bonds; and district notifications regarding indebtedness to participate in OFCC projects as required in 133.06(I). Files may include approval or disapproval documentation, applications requesting consent, request letters or email correspondence, resolution of necessity, resolution to proceed, net indebtedness statement (DTE form 131), statement of facts, Co. Auditors mileage certificate (DTE form 130), and the certification of school board members. Special needs designation includes special needs resolution, tax valuation (history and projection of growth), the projected needs and the estimated cost of permanent improvements.
retention_statement: Electronic: Where consent and notifications relate to an election date, retain five (5) years from date of the election ; Paper: Where consent and notifications relate to an election date, retain five (5) years from date of the election

series_description: Post-Secondary Enrollment Options (PSEO) is a program established to permit high school students in grades nine through twelve to earn college and high school graduation credit through the completion of college courses. This includes the forms SF-PS-140A and SF-PS-140B.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years ; Electronic: Retain five years

series_description: These records include both intradistrict and interdistrict policies on open enrollment which have been submitted to the Ohio Department of Education.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain two years ; Paper: Retain two years

series_description: The Auxiliary Services Program is authorized under 3317.06 and 3317.024 of the Ohio Revised Code. Payments are made on a per pupil basis to the public school district where the non-public school is located. The public school district, upon requests from the non-public school, provides 1) textbooks, 2) diagnostic, therapeutic, and remedial personnel services, and 3) educational equipment to the non-public school students. This includes forms SF-230 and SF-200.
retention_statement: Paper: Five years, provided the records have been audited by the Auditor of State and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved. ; Electronic: Five years, provided the records have been audited by the Auditor of State and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_description: Funds available for the repair, relocation, or replacement of Auxiliary Service mobile units upon demonstration of need and approval from the Department of Education. This includes forms SF-238 and SF-239.
retention_statement: Paper: Five years, provided the records have been audited by the Auditor of State and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved. ; Electronic: Five years, provided the records have been audited by the Auditor of State and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_description: Annual reimbursement provided to each chartered nonpublic school for the administrative and clerical costs incurred during the preceding school year. This includes form SF-240.
retention_statement: Electronic: Five years, provided the records have been audited by the Auditor of State and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved. ; Paper: Five years, provided the records have been audited by the Auditor of State and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_description: Tuition is an amount of money paid to an educating district from a resident district to offset the educating district’s cost of educating a student who is a legal resident of another school district. This includes forms SF-14 (Tuition Adjustment Expenditure), SF-14H (Tuition Adjustment), SF-14Y (Department of Youth Services), SF-DRC (District of Residence Change) Form, MO-8 and SF-14PD (Private Treatment Facilities). Some of these forms are accessible through the Ohio District Data Exchange (ODDEX) system.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years ; Electronic: Retain 5 years

series_title: District fiscal monitoring files (Management Assistant Reviews, "MARS")
series_description: District educational financial records regarding how a district spends its special education funding.
retention_statement: Paper: Records must be maintained until the next regular audit by the Auditor of State. Once the audit has been performed, the records can be destroyed.

series_description: Application, correspondence, and any other documents related to an application for a community school seeking sponsorship from the Office of School Sponsorship.
retention_statement: Electronic: One year from the date of the denial of the application ; Paper: One year from the date of the denial of the application

series_title: School File for Sponsored Community Schools
series_description: Contract, application correspondence, compliance documents and any other documents related to the sponsorship of a community school by the Office of School Sponsorship.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain two years from the date of the expiration of Office of School Sponsorship, which may include non-renewal, termination, transfer or closure. ; Electronic: Retain two years from the date of the expiration of Office of School Sponsorship, which may include non-renewal, termination, transfer or closure

series_title: Student Files Log Sheet from Closed Community Schools
series_description: When a community school closes the Office of School Sponsorship takes temporary custody of these files and then distributes them to the students'' districts of residence. This log records the distribution of these files.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain twenty years.

series_description: Progress reports regarding Office of Communications projects, both general and issue-specific. Examples would include planning agendas, dashboard and newsletter traffic, and open-rate reporting.
retention_statement: Electronic: Three years

series_description: This report contains requests from individuals with diabetes whom want to become a school bus driver. Before consent from ODE, the following medical documentation must be submitted and approved: vital statistics, copy of valid commercial driver license, 3 year driving history (abstract), completed t-8 (3 pages), documentation and approval from Endocrinologist on driver history, medical history, documentation and approval by an Opthamologist, and either the approval or disapproval from ODE. Follow-up documentation for 3 years by Opthamologist and Endocrinologist @ 6 months intervals.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for the longevity of the driver's service. ; Electronic: Retain for the longevity of the driver's service.

series_description: These manuals are created for the Districts to follow policies and procedures for state testing: Directions for Administration; District Test Coordinator; Building Test Coordinator Manuals; Supplemental Instructions for Paper Testing; and Test Administration Manuals. These manuals are updated annually for every Fall, Spring and optional summer testing for each school year.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years ; Electronic: Retain five years

series_description: Secured system that requires username and password for ODE staff that works in collaborations with the AIR (American Institute for Research) secure system. This collaborative platform is used for all test development client’s items and other materials used during Item Batch Reviews, web previews, etc. This system is used to write tests then communicate with vendor for feedback until item reviews are satisfactory. The item reviews are stored in the repository of the AIR system until field testing is completed, which at that point, the tests cannot be modified, altered or deleted.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for five (5) years after contract with vendor is expired, audited by Auditor of State, and audit report is released, and all discrepancies are resolved

series_description: The CD is created to use for students who are vision impaired and the test recorded audibly to read the questions on the test to the student.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for five (5) years

series_description: The CD is created to use for foreign students and the test are recorded audibly in a different language for the student.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for five (5) years

series_description: Ohio State's Test Rules Books indicating who has to take the test and what rules to follow to adhere to the entire assessment program. The General Rules Book is updated annually in September.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years ; Electronic: Retain five years

series_description: Blank Test Books without student responses. There may be up to 10 forms of each paper test and special version forms.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for five (5) years after administration of test. Review by State before disposa ; Electronic: Retain for five (5) years after administration of test. Review by State Archives before disposa

series_title: Appointment and Application Materials for Advisory Council, Grant Advisors, and Scorers- CC
series_description: Documents submitted by the members of the advisory council, grant advisors, and scorers: including but not limited to applications, resumes, and conflict of interest forms. Training materials distributed to advisory council, grant advisors, and scorers.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years. ; Electronic: Retain 5 years.

series_title: Community Connectors Grant Applications and Scoring Documents
series_description: Grant application materials and scoring documents for the Community Connectors.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years ; Electronic: Retain 5 years

series_description: The adopted minutes of Community Connectors Advisory Board meetings
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years; ; Electronic: Retain 5 years;

series_description: Includes correspondence, meeting minutes, materials, standards rubrics, assessment rubrics, and other supporting data not listed.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 6 years. Review by State Archives before disposal. ; Electronic: Retain for 6 years. Review by State Archives before disposal.

series_description: Applications to become a provider to direct or supervise a pupil activity program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years, if paper copy is scanned into a computer database, ensure quality and completeness before discarding the paper. ; Electronic: Retain 5 years

series_description: Applications submitted for a nomination to become a member of the Credential Review Board. Members of the Credentials Review Board shall assess the credentials of individuals pursuing alternative routes to Educator Licensure and out of state Educators seeking licensure in Ohio according to O.R.C. 3319.65.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years, if application is scanned into a computer system, ensure its quality and completeness before discarding the paper. ; Electronic: Retain 5 years

series_description: Includes, appointment letters, working papers for an external audit, and the final copy of the FTE Reviews of the Community Schools conducted by the Area Coordinators.
retention_statement: Paper: Recommended Retention PeriodAfter final review, all records shall be stored at the Ohio Department of Education (central office) for the remainder of the retention period. Retain six (6) years after final review conducted by Area Coordinators ; Electronic: Recommended Retention PeriodAfter final review, all records shall be stored at the Ohio Department of Education (central office) for the remainder of the retention period. Retain six (6) years after final review conducted by Area Coordinators

series_description: The Catastrophic Cost program is used by traditional school districts, community schools and joint vocational schools to submit reimbursement requests for costs exceeding the threshold to educated K-12 students with disabilities in Categories 2-6. This reimbursement is available to the financially responsible district.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years, provided the records have been audited by the Auditor of State and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved. ; Electronic: 5 years, provided the records have been audited by the Auditor of State and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_description: Yearly listing of Approved Textbook Publishers and required information submitted by them.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years ; Electronic: 5 years

series_description: Blank Answer Documents without student responses. Blank Answer documents do not contain written responses from students. The documents contain ten (10) versions of the answer documents and includes versions for the vision impaired and hearing impaired.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five (5) years after administration of the answer documents. Retain one for each grade level per subject level for each administration year. Review by State before disposal. ; Electronic: Retain five (5) years after administration of the answer documents. Retain one for each grade level per subject level for each administration year. Review by State Archives before disposal.

series_description: Original office files containing documentation and the decision of the Superintendent of Public Instruction pertaining to a dispute between a family and a school district as to the school district of residence for the family.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 10 years ; Paper: Retain 10 years

series_description: Files developed during investigations of known or alleged fraud, abuse, and irregularities or violations of laws and regulations. This record series includes, but not limited to; investigative files relating to employee, complaints, state audit referrals, and external compliant referrals, etc. These files consist of investigative reports and related documents, such as correspondence, subpoenas, notes, attachments, and working papers.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for five (5) federal fiscal years from the date the investigation is completed ; Paper: Retain for five (5) federal fiscal years from the date the investigation is completed

series_description: Compliance documentation required by local school districts, community schools and non-public schools to submit calamity day plans and resolutions approving the plan passed by their board of education or governing authority, which is submitted to the Ohio Department of Education for the applicable school year. The calamity day plans require educators to use online lessons or “Blizzard Bags” to make up the number of hours equivalent to three (3) school days.
retention_statement: Electronic: Destroy/delete when 2 years old.

series_description: Records maintained by ODE’s Compliance Officer relating to the training and compliance of proposed or established executive branch standards of ethical conduct and other ethics regulations; conflict of interest and other ethics related statutes and Executive Orders; and any agency supplemental standards of ethical conduct and other agency ethics-related regulations and directives.
retention_statement: Electronic: Destroy when 3 years old or when superseded or obsolete, whichever is later.Duplicates: Retain until obsolete, superseded or administrative value is lost.

series_description: Includes, but is not limited to, meeting subject files, applications, supporting documentation, staff write up and reviews, presentation materials and resolutions, minutes, and other related documents.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office until the end of board member’s term. Review by State before disposal.

series_description: This record series includes reports on agency activities required by
retention_statement: Electronic: Upon publication, fulfill the requirements of O.R.C. 149.11. Destroy excess paper copies when no longer of administrative value.

series_description: This record series includes non-compliance and compliance field reports by personnel of school visitation and other activities to identify innovations, review compliance, and for research purposes throughout schools and districts.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years ; Paper: Retain 2 years

series_title: Superintendent's Work Group for Drop Out Prevention & Recovery
series_description: This record series includes correspondence, research, information and materials to support the efforts of the work group, which demonstrates effective, sustainable, and coordinated dropout prevention and re-entry programs.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 3 years

series_description: This records series includes case management files, applications, supplemental materials, site reviews, site visits, survey data, general materials, on-site summary, and addendum of applications.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years after the close of the grant; provided the records have been audited by the Auditor of State and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_description: This records series includes On-site Review Files which contains the date of on-site reviews, conducted in schools and districts, draft and final review reports, correspondence regarding the review, and clearance letters.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five (5) years after grant closure; provided the records have been audited by the Auditor of State and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved. ; Electronic: Retain five (5) years after grant closure; provided the records have been audited by the Auditor of State and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_title: Board Member Information File- Straight A Fund
series_description: Files relating to the Straight A Fund Governing Board members, including but not limited to resumes, biographies, and contact information.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for duration of member’s term;

series_description: Ohio Department of Education website pages contain information made available to the public via the Internet as a matter of convenience. This records series may include, but are not limited to, general descriptive information about the State Board of Education Meetings, Administrators, Educators, Community, Agency Program groups, Frequently Asked Questions, Media News Center, bulletins, sample forms, weekly announcements, annual reports, rules of practice and procedure, etc. This Information is maintained electronically or in paper format by the record owners under separate retention schedules.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain on-line until content information is obsolete, replaced, or superseded. After the content information is obsolete, replaced, and/or superseded; a document control log is maintained to capture what information was made available to the public, when it was presented on-line and when it was removed.

series_description: Completive scholarships awarded to graduating seniors lo be used for undergraduate studies to recognize exceptionally able high school seniors who show promise of continued excellence in post secondary education.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years, provided the records have been audited by the Auditor of State and the Auditor of the Federal Government;

series_description: When a school district educates a special needs student who is not a resident of the district, that district may charge the district of residence “excess costs”. Excess costs are defined as the cost to educate the student minus the amount of State and local funds received for educating the student. Districts incurring excess costs may seek reimbursement after the end of the fiscal year by filing an excess cost application and providing supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years, provided audits by the Auditor of State have been conducted, audit reports released, and audit resolutions issued or resolved. ; Electronic: 5 years, provided audits by the Auditor of State have been conducted, audit reports released, and audit resolutions issued or resolved.

series_description: Forms submitted by private facilities that provide services to students placed at the facility. This includes forms SF-14P, SF-6P, ADM-1-P Actual and ADM-1-P Estimate.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years, provided the state auditor has conducted an audit, the audit reports are released, and audit issues are resolved ; Electronic: Retain for 5 years, provided the state auditor, has conducted an audit, the audit reports are released, and audit issues are resolved

series_description: Includes surveys and studies conducted by the P-20 Safety and Security office to statutorily mandate educational facilities to submit an emergency management plan, in order to determine their compliance with
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years ; Electronic: Retain 3 years

series_description: Reports sent to the Governor concerning business activities of the Office of P-20 Safety and Security. Reports may include, but not limited to information regarding school safety, the Toll-free Tip-line calls or texts about Bullying, notifications, etc.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years ; Electronic: Retain 3 years

series_title: Safety Training for Administrative School Staff
series_description: This record series includes training materials for the following topics: Safety Awareness, Child Abuse, Human Trafficking, Alcohol Abuse, Cyber Bullying, and Bullying. Safe and supportive materials are included with these records.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years. Review by State prior to disposal. ; Electronic: Retain 5 years. Review by State Archives prior to disposal.

series_description: Required documents,in which the school districts must submit their emergency management plans to the Ohio Department of Education, to maintain their compliance in safety regulations. Every 3 years the plans expire and must be re-evaluated, reviewed and the final plan is approved and stored in the ''SAFE'' accounts database.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 6 years after the initial compliance of the emergency management plan ; Electronic: Retain 6 years after the initial compliance of the emergency management plan

series_description: Records includes, but are not limited to; the documentation of telephone calls, texts and/or emails to prevent and alert law enforcement to a school safety crisis. The electronic file or log is created to monitor Information received regarding school safety plans, better learning environments, school fire, safety drills, and the tip line information.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 1 year ; Electronic: Retain 1 year

series_description: Include local school districts approved administrative procedures and correspondence regarding review and approval of the procedures.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain each local school district''s approved administrative procedures in the office until replaced by the submission of new procedures.

series_description: This records series contains all written agreements with ODE, including Contracts, Memorandum of Understanding (MOU), Inter-agency Agreements, Win-Win Agreements, Personal Service Contracts or any such document where a vendor, entity or party enters into a written, expressed contract with ODE wherein all parties involved have signed to demonstrate they agree with all stated provisions, ideas, and stipulations. This series also includes the ODE Agency Approvals/Denials Signature page and correspondence with the vendor.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in operating area until imaged and indexed. Upon confirmation that paper copy has been checked for quality and completeness ; Electronic: Retain 8 years from the date of expiration or cancellation per ORC 2305.6.

series_title: Meeting Materials from official State Board of Education meetings.
series_description: This file contains all State Board of Education and committee meeting materials distributed and received from official meetings of the State Board of Education. Materials are used for responding to public records requests and research by Department of Education personnel.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 10 years

series_title: Public Records Request and Response Files
series_description: Requests from the public for Ohio Department of Education records and the corresponding responses. Documents may include, but not limited to the original request, correspondence between staff and the requestor or other staff; redacted and unredacted responsive records. Maintained to document compliance with public records law obligations (
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one year ; Electronic: Retain one year

series_description: Master roster of public records requests
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain on shared drive for a period of one calendar year

series_description: Contain correspondence and other pertinent information relative to direct facilitation and direct mediation requested involving the provision of special education programs and services to suspected students with disabilities and students with suspected disabilities.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three (3) years after case is closed ; Electronic: Retain three (3) years after case is closed

series_description: Includes Reports and Action Plans as a result of the monitoring review. Once review is closed, the file will contain all transient records; district self-reports, any district documents sent for review,and any previous review materials on file.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain reports and action plans for five (5) years ; Electronic: Retain reports and action plans for five (5) years

series_description: This form is used by Assessment Committee members that have signed a Non-Disclosure form, to keep information confidential with test review information.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain five years ; Paper: Retain five years

series_description: Include project applications, fiscal reports, activity reports, on site monitoring reports, correspondence regarding projects and child count data for Title VI-B projects.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain three years, after grant closure. provided audit has been conducted by Auditor of State, the audit report is released, and all discrepancies areresolved. ; Paper: Retain three years, after grant closure. provided audit has been conducted by Auditor of State, the audit report is released, and all discrepancies areresolved.

series_description: Log used to document the Anonymous Parent Satisfaction Surveys received in paper for special education in Ohio districts.
retention_statement: Paper: Anonymous Parent Satisfaction are transient records. Recycle immediately after data from survey is entered into Indicator 8 spreadsheet. ; Electronic: Retain electronic log for five years

series_title: State Advisory Panel for Exceptional Children (SAPEC)
series_description: Includes minutes of meetings, published notice and annual report issued by state advisory council, which is operated in accordance with the requirements of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for ten(10) years ; Electronic: Retain for ten(10) years

series_title: State Plan for Education of Exceptional Children
series_description: State Performance Plan Annual Progress Report (SPP-APR) developed and submitted by the State Educational Agency in accordance with
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 10 years ; Electronic: Retain for 10 years

series_description: The Entry Year Teacher Program documents includes files that contain fiscal information ( i.e. budgets, contracts, agreements, requisitions, and cash report, and evaluations).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three (3) years after completion of program

series_description: Tracks process of payment in lieu of providing transportation services. This record is for students that were declared eligible but impractical for transportation services according to revised code 3327.02
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years, provided the records have been audited by the Auditor of State and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved. ; Paper: Retain 5 years, provided the records have been audited by the Auditor of State and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_description: All records concerning federal grants to state agencies which document the expenditure of federal monies. Can include, but may not be limited to, grant applications and supporting documentation, financial reports and monitoring reports.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 3 biennia plus current year (total 8 years) and until audited by State Auditor, audit report released, and audit resolutions issued or resolved. ; Electronic: Retain for 3 biennia plus current year (total 8 years) and until audited by State Auditor, audit report released, and audit resolutions issued or resolved.

series_description: Data collection document that contains the fall enrollment of each public and non-public school building in Ohio. Enrollment is provided by gender within grade. Ethnic and county of residence counts are also included. Data is used to determine the amount of funds distributed to nonpublic schools for programs such as Auxiliary Services and Administrative Cost Reimbursement pursuant to O.R.C. 3317.06.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one year after collection ; Electronic: Retain one year after collection

series_description: Description of enacted legislation, including synopsis, stakeholders and vote breakdown
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain through the current General assembly and one additional General Assembly

series_description: This record series may include but not limited to; correspondence, letters, graders, scoring sheets, grant applications, original grant, and approval notifications.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for three (3) years after the grant has closed; provided the Federal audit is closed without discrepancy. ; Paper: Retain for three (3) years after the grant has closed; provided the Federal audit is closed without discrepancy.

series_description: This record series may include but not limited to; documentation regarding the community school, their sponsor, sponsor assurances, name changes, suspensions, field reports, forms and correspondences.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for five years after the school has closed. ; Paper: Retain for five years after the school has closed.

series_description: Original office files containing documentation on specific issues addressed by the Legal Office on behalf of the Ohio Department of Education and the State Board of Education.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for five (5) years ; Electronic: Retain for five (5) years

series_description: This record series includes educator evaluation reports measuring high-quality student data to demonstrate student learning, student academic growth data and instruction to meet individual student needs and improve practice.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years ; Electronic: Retain five years

series_description: This record series includes agendas, minutes, supporting records documenting internal policy decisions for the STEM Committee, which focuses on approval of designation of STEM Schools.
retention_statement: Paper: The meeting minutes shall be transferred to the State Archives. ; Electronic: Retain five (5) years

series_title: STEM (Science Technology Engineering Mathematics) school application
series_description: This record series includes applications for approval to become a STEM school that are required to be submitted to the Ohio Department of Education’s STEM Committee, as established by section 3326.03 and 3326.032 of the Revised Code
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain five (5) years after school closes or after the school designation has been revoked, provided the records have been audited by the Auditor of State and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved.Duplicates: Retain until obsolete, superseded or administrative value is lost.

series_description: School district applications for new funded classroom units through the school foundation program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years and ; Electronic: Retain 5 years and

series_title: Report-Recipients and Uses of Special Accounts & Special Purpose Accounts
series_description: This series contains the recipients and uses of all GRF special purpose line items and all other funds administered by the department of the previous fiscal year. These reports are required by the legislature. "By the thirtieth day of August of each year, The ODE shall report to to the President of the Senate, the Speaker of the House of Representatives, the Director of Budget & Mgmt, the Legislative Budget Office of Education Oversight, and the Legislative Budget office the recipients and uses of all GRF special purpose line items and all other funds administered by the department of the previous fiscal year..."
retention_statement: Paper: Retain two (2) years ; Electronic: Retain two (2) years

series_description: Form SF-230 Supplement is provided for Public School Officials who sent an overpayment to the Treasurer, State of Ohio and desire a refund of money not to exceed the amount of insufficiency from the Bureau of Unemployment Services as provided in Section 4141.47 ORC and certification by the Department of Education as required in such section.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years ; Electronic: Retain five years

series_description: Form SF-238 is used to request approval for the replacement of auxiliary service mobile units.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years ; Electronic: Retain five years

series_title: Educational Cost - Juvenile Court Subsidy Claim
series_description: Documentation provided for use by the courts in Ohio to certify to the Ohio Department of Education, Division of Field Relations, the daily per capita cost of each student placed by the court in a private institution or facility, and for the purpose of claiming the subsidy provided for in Section 2151.35.7 O.R.C.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years ; Electronic: Retain 5 years

series_description: These reports (T-1 form) created in the School Foundation Payment system and used by school district requesting reimbursement for transporting students. Categories of information include numbers of buses by model year and type, public and non-public pupils, grade level, itemized expenses, special data, and school location.
retention_statement: Electronic: Data is entered into the Department computer, retained for 10 years, and ; Paper: Paper maintained in Pupil Transportation section files for 2 years.

series_title: School Bus Driver Medical Examination File
series_description: This report (T-8 form) contains the results of a medical examination by a physician, including such categories as loss of limb, blood pressure, drug use, tuberculosis, vision, hearing, heart, emotional stability, etc. Also included are addresses for the driver.
retention_statement: Electronic: Data is entered into the department computer system, retain for 10 years and ; Paper: Retain for ten years

series_description: This report (T-8 form) contains detailed information about accidents involving school buses, including driver and vehicle particulars. Three major segments include how the school bus was physically involved, details of the loading/unloading zone, and an injury tally chart.
retention_statement: Electronic: Data is entered into the department computer system, retain for 5 years and ; Paper: Retain five years

series_description: Driver Education Out-of-State Transfer Certificate are issued to any person under the age of 18 who has completed a driver education program from another state and who will be residing in the State of Ohio. These files contain copies of both out-of-state certificates and out-of state transfer certificates.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in the Division of School Finance for two fiscal years. Then, send to storage for eight (8) years before . ; Electronic: Retain for 10 years

series_description: This report contains data from each school district that has been furnished with a Multi-Media System funded by the National Highway Safety Administration for use in Driver Education classes. This teaching aide is a student response system with Multi-Media Controls which make possible the use of automatically programmed audio and/or visual presentations in addition to provideing the instruction, the means to monitor student comprehension during the instruction process.
retention_statement: Paper: Retained these originals in Division until ownership is transferred ; Electronic: Maintain these originals until ownership is transferred

series_description: Competitive Teacher Preparation & Retention (f.k.a. Personnel Development Center) proposals received from Ohio public universities to train vocational education teachers.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years and ; Electronic: Retain 5 years and

series_title: Comprehensive Annual Report for Administration of the Perkins Grant.
series_description: Annual Performance Reports for Vocational Education.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years and ; Electronic: Retain 5 years

series_description: Waiver applications and award letters for the Innovative Education Pilot Program established in Ohio Revised Code (ORC)3302.07 and Ohio Administrative Code (OAC)3301-46-01.
retention_statement: Paper: Five (5) years ; Electronic: Five (5) years

series_description: Records pertaining to the children residing at the Ohio Veterans Children's Home, previously known as the Ohio Soldiers’ and Sailors’ Orphans Home. These records include education and residential information.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 100 years from the birth date of the child residing at the home. Some files were digitized, yet the originals are still in paper format. The enrollment cards and the applications for admissions will be pulled out of the files and transferred to Greene County Library in Xenia, OH. ; Electronic: For the electronic format, retain for 100 years from the birth date of the child residing at the home.

series_description: Office files pertaining to a schools appeal concerning participation in school lunch and child nutrition programs.
retention_statement: Paper: When paper is converted to electronic format, check for accuracy and completeness ; Electronic: Retain 5 years

series_description: Folders containing Emergency School Advancement Loan (ESAL) applications, support documents, loan approval and receipt, repayment schedules, monitoring reports, and necessary notes and communication 3313.483 ORC
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years ; Electronic: Retain five years

series_description: Actual request and approval application for fiscal year end borrowing by public school districts in Ohio against future taxes; approval by the Superintendent of Public Instruction of such borrowing pursuant to Section 133.301 of the Ohio Revised Code.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 10 years

series_description: Unique individual student electronic records containing State Student Identification (SSID), SS#, Parent identification, Parent SS#, DOB, address, etc. These records may include, but not limited to the: EdChoice Scholarship; Autism Scholarship; Cleveland Scholarship and/or the Jon Peterson Scholarships. These records provide verification of where student is attending school, where he attended in the past. This verification is necessary to program staff in determining eligibility to receive the scholarship and payment. They also provide a tracking mechanism for address verification. This scholarship is based on where the student resides. These records will need to be retained from kindergarten to graduation. All records are electronic. These student records are accessible to the program staff to resolve residency issues, school attending, transferred students, testing issues, etc. The records are maintained with the application to determine resolution regarding disbursements of school payments or any discrepancies.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain five years

series_title: Ohio Veterans Children's Home (OVCH) Medical Records
series_description: Medical records pertaining to the children residing at the Ohio Veterans Children's Home, previously known as the Ohio Soldiers’ and Sailors’ Orphans Home.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper files for 50 years after the date of birth for the child residing at the home. Some files were digitized; yet the originals are still in paper format. For the electronic format, retain for 50 years after the date of birth for the child residing at the home. ; Electronic: Retain paper files for 50 years after the date of birth for the child residing at the home. Some files were digitized; yet the originals are still in paper format.For the electronic format, retain for 50 years after the date of birth for the child residing at the home.

series_description: These are original case files pertaining to any litigation that ODE is involved in. These contain briefs, subpoenas, memos and other filings as well as correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 20 years after case is closed. ; Electronic: Retain for 20 years after case is closed.

series_description: Office files relating to requests that a student be permanently excluded from attendance at any public school in Ohio. (
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 25 years ; Electronic: Retain 25 years

series_description: Includes worksheets and information used to draft rules to the Joint Committee on Agency Rule Review (JCARR) procedings, transcripts of public hearings.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain for 6 years from effective date of rule and destroy. ; Electronic: Maintain for 6 years from effective date of rule and delete.

series_description: Drafts and final versions of proposed legislative language.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain through the current General assembly and one additional General Assembly

series_description: Spreadsheet detailing amendments and possible amendments to legislation
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain through the current General assembly and one additional General Assembly

series_description: Contact lists, including mailing address, telephone number and email address of education stakeholders.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded

series_description: Copies of testimony given by Ohio Department of Education (ODE) staff to legislative committees.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain through the current General Assembly, plus two additional General Assemblies

series_description: Any introduced or proposed legislation
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain through the current General assembly and one additional General Assembly

series_title: Weekly reports to Governor/State Board of Education
series_description: Required weekly report submitted to Governor’s office and/or State Board of Education
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain through the current General Assembly, plus 6 months.

series_description: Files contain documentation concerning school districts under the fiscal oversight status of Fiscal Emergency. Files may include requests for solvency assistance, repayment schedules, financial forecasts by the Auditor of State's office, correspondence, and all Financial Supervision and Planning Commission materials (including financial recovery plans and addendums, meeting agenda's, resolutions passed, meeting minutes, and monitoring reports, etc.), and release documentation.
retention_statement: Electronic: All records will be retained during the fiscal emergency period. Upon release from fiscal emergency and disbanding of the commission, records will be transferred from the Ohio Department of Education to the Auditor of State as pursuant to ORC 3316.16(C). ; Paper: All records will be retained during the fiscal emergency period. Upon release from fiscal emergency and disbanding of the commission, records will be transferred from the Ohio Department of Education to the Auditor of State as pursuant to ORC 3316.16(C).

series_description: Data, reports, contracts, school information, and other documents requested as part of the Sponsor Performance Review.
retention_statement: Paper: Ten years, provided the records have been audited by the Auditor of State and the report is released and all discrepancies are resolved. ; Electronic: Ten years, provided the records have been audited by the Auditor of State and the report is released and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_description: This record series contains the five-year projection forms based on anticipated revenue, expenditures, changes in balances of foundation payments forecasted for each community school.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for five years

series_title: Administrative Records from a Closed Community School
series_description: When a Community School sponsored by the Office of School Sponsorship closes, all of the administrative records of the school are transferred to the Office School Sponsorship. These include, but are not limited to: records pertaining to employees; food services; transportation; finances; and other administrative functions and/or services of the school.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for three (3) years from the date of closure or until final audit is complete (whichever is later) ; Paper: Retain for three (3) years from the date of closure or until final audit is complete (whichever is later)

series_description: Files with requests to transfer school district or ESC territory, create a new school district or alter school district boundaries
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for ten (10) years ; Paper: Retain for ten (10) years

series_description: In addition to the application form and payment, High School Equivalence (GED) applications may include, but are not limited to, age waivers, court orders, parental consent forms, guardianship documentation and official high school transcript documenting the previous 12 months of applicant's enrollment in high school diploma program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for for Two (2) years ; Electronic: Retain for for Two (2) years

series_title: Licensure applications and supporting eligibility documentation
series_description: Licensure applications and supporting eligibility documentation regarding approval or denial of the administrative license, permit or certification.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for ten (10) years ; Paper: Retain for ten (10) years

series_description: The Resident Educator Summative Assessment consists of five tasks which teachers must complete in order to complete the Ohio Resident Educator program and qualify for a professional license. These files contain the candidates''task submissions, including forms, instructional evidence, and videos.
retention_statement: Electronic: If professional license is awarded

series_description: Includes appointment letters, working papers for Single audits, on-site and desk reviews, and closure letters and reports.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain six years after final grant activity ; Electronic: Retain six years after final grant activity

series_description: These files contain documentation prepared to substantiate proper departmental cost allocation plans of Federal Awards based on cash expenditures of the previous fiscal year activities and the projecting costs for the billing year, in accordance with Office of Management and Budget (OMB). These proposal records are submitted by the grantee annually.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for three years, provided the Federal Audit has been conducted by the Federal Government and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved ; Electronic: Retain for three years, provided the Federal Audit has been conducted by the Federal Government and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved

series_description: Routine correspondence pertaining to the day-to-day activities of the office, including: internal correspondence (letters, memos, emails); correspondence from various individuals, companies and organizations requesting information pertaining to agency and legal interpretations; requests for routine information or publications provided to the public by an agency which are answered by standard form letters; and other miscellaneous inquires. This correspondence is informative (it does not attempt to influence agency policy).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper in operating area until imaged and indexed. Once confirmed the paper copy has been checked for accuracy, quality, and completeness ; Electronic: Retain the electronic record for 1 year;

series_title: Supporting Documentation for Facilitation of Teaching Certificates for Prison Teachers
series_description: These records are files associated with the facilitation of teacher certifications for teachers who work in prisons. Aspiring teachers send supporting documentation which may include a history of employment, contact information, high school diploma, transcripts, etc.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper in operating area until imaged and indexed. Once confirmed the paper copy has been checked for accuracy, quality, and completeness ; Electronic: Retain the electronic record for 1 year;

series_title: Gifted and talented local education agency policies
series_description: District acceleration policies, parent notification procedures for identification of gifted and talented children and possible provision of services, and method for identifying gifted and talented students.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain the electronic record for 5 years; ; Paper: Retain paper in operating area until imaged and indexed. Once confirmed the paper copy has been checked for accuracy, quality, and completeness

series_title: Waivers for case-load ratios and classroom size
series_description: Educational agency requests and documentation to waive special education classroom and educator case load ratio work requirements.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper in operating area until imaged and indexed. Once confirmed the paper copy has been checked for accuracy, quality, and completeness ; Electronic: Retain the electronic record for three (3) years;

series_description: Records submitted by ombudsman detailing complaints from the public and attempts to resolve issues. This records series includes the logs of telephone calls between outside callers and the department''s ombudsmen. These logs record the main issue or question discussed and the time taken to resolve the call.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper in operating area until imaged and indexed. Once confirmed the paper copy has been checked for accuracy, quality, and completeness ; Electronic: Retain the electronic record for 1 year;

series_description: Meeting minutes, agendas, project documents and final reports of the School Funding Advisory Council (SFAC) with respect to the recommendations of Ohio's evidence-based school funding system. Subcommittees established to foster collaboration and reported its findings and recommendations to the council.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 3 years ; Paper: Retain for 3 years

series_title: Requests for Authorization to Issue Obligations
series_description: Requests from school districts for consent to issue obligations not covered in other retention schedules. These include but are not limited to tax anticipation notes in accordance with
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain records for the life of the obligation if approved. If unapproved, retain for five (5) years. ; Paper: Retain records for the life of the obligation if approved. If unapproved, retain for five (5) years.

series_description: Open and closed case files may contain, but not limited to the following documents; reference documents created and maintained in the Professional Conduct Case Management (PCCM) system (i.e. reports relating to BCI/FBI, School district referrals, demographics, credentials, etc.); Investigation reports and documentation; records produced as a result of an investigation (i.e. personnel files, court and police records, children service reports, telephone records, etc.) Notes; Case recommendations and non-Disciplinary Case completion records, and administrative documentation,).
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for the life of the Educator's career. ; Paper: Paper records shredded after being scanned and uploaded to the office’s case management system or office shared drive for larger files (e.g. video and audio files, phone records).

series_description: These records are documentation of a Non-Chartered Non-Tax Records. These school records indicate how many years they have been listed with ODE as a (08) Non-Chartered, Non-tax supported), school, etc.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain five years ; Paper: Retain five years

series_title: Fiscal Oversight- Fiscal Caution and Fiscal Watch
series_description: Files contain documentation concerning school districts under fiscal oversight status of Fiscal Caution or Fiscal Watch. Files may include designation of Fiscal Caution or Fiscal Watch status, correspondence, written proposals to eliminate deficits, financial recovery plans, monitoring reports, etc., and release from Fiscal Caution or Fiscal Watch status.
retention_statement: Electronic: For oversight period and not to exceed five (5) years ; Paper: For oversight period and not to exceed five (5) years

series_description: Request from school districts to participate in the state credit enhancement program pursuant to
retention_statement: Electronic: For the life of the bond registrar agreement, if approved. If unapproved, retain for five (5) years. ; Paper: For the life of the bond registrar agreement, if approved. If unapproved, retain for five (5) years.

series_description: Files concerning motor vehicles owned/leased by the Ohio Department of Education (ODE). Records include, but are not limited to, service records, operation maintenance reports of vehicle, operating manual, repair records, and salvage information.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one (1) year, after vehicle is salvaged or sold ; Electronic: Retain one (1) year, after vehicle is salvaged or sold

series_description: This record series contains orders from a court, an attorney, or other authoritative entity that commands appearance and/or testimony in court, at hearing, or at deposition, or commands production of documents or other items.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain subpoenas that command appearance until fulfilled. Retain all other subpoenas and responsive records for six (6) months from date of compliance. ; Electronic: Retain subpoenas that command appearance until fulfilled. Retain all other subpoenas and responsive records for six (6) months from date of compliance

series_title: Civil Rights and Title VII Files
series_description: Documents containing complaints and hearing information.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain seven (7) years from settlement or case closed ; Electronic: Retain seven (7) years from settlement or case closed

series_description: Records posted to the ED STEPS Guidance Library include planning records for goals, strategies, action steps and district goal amounts for all grants in the ED STEPS System. The Funding Applications included are Consolidated, Competitive, Student Intervention, Career-Technical and Adult Education, Adult Basic and Literacy Education and Community School. Other documents include budgets, budget details, nonpublic services and other related pages.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until information becomes obsolete, superseded, or until no longer of administrative or research value

series_description: Records consist of all information, decisions or documentation related to a specific grant program competition or a group of multiple competitions within a grant program competition. Files may include, but not limited to; program regulations, relevant statutes, review agreements, review package and waivers, application technical review plans, funding slates, and other documents.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 15 years after final action is taken on file. ; Electronic: Retain for 15 years after final action is taken on file.

series_description: The discretionary grant case file includes the original application, required forms, grant award notifications, annual grant performance reports, correspondence, decisions or any document that has been identified as part of the official grant file
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 15 years after final action is taken on file. ; Paper: Retain for 15 years after final action is taken on file.

series_description: Elementary and Secondary Education Act (ESEA) waiver includes the documentation for districts to have flexibility from sanctions and reporting requirements to increase the quality of instruction for students and improve the academic achievement of students.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 15 years after final action is taken on file.

series_title: Every Student Succeeds Act (ESSA) Mandated Committee
series_description: This records series includes documentation of activities performed by the ESSA Mandated Committee. These documents include meeting agendas, committee rosters, committee member applications and selection rubrics.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 15 years after final action is taken on file.

series_description: This records series includes documentation of various grant funds used to leverage resources and the flexibility of funds to upgrade the entire educational program of a school.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 15 years after final action is taken on file.

series_title: (CCIP) Comprehensive Continuous Improvement Plan System Records
series_description: Records created in the grant application & verification system includes planning records for goals, strategies, action steps and district goal amounts for all grants in the CCIP.
